description: Low-level networking interface. Provides access to the BSD socket interface.

functions.name: socket

params.name: family
params.description: Address family. Common values: AF_INET (IPv4, default), AF_INET6 (IPv6), AF_UNIX (Unix domain).

params.name: type
params.description: Socket type. Common values: SOCK_STREAM (TCP, default), SOCK_DGRAM (UDP), SOCK_RAW.

params.name: proto
params.description: Protocol number. Usually 0, meaning the default protocol for the address family and type.

returns.description: A new socket object.

functions.description: Create a new socket using the given address family, socket type, and protocol number.

functions.name: create_connection

params.name: address
params.description: A (host, port) tuple for the remote address to connect to.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Timeout in seconds for the connection attempt. None means use the global default.

params.name: source_address
params.description: A (host, port) tuple to bind to as the source address before connecting.

returns.description: A connected socket object.

functions.description: Convenience function that creates a TCP connection to a remote address and returns the socket object. Tries all addresses returned by getaddrinfo().

functions.name: getaddrinfo
functions.signature: getaddrinfo(host, port[, family[, socktype[, proto[, flags]]]])

params.name: host
params.description: The host name or IP address. None means any.

params.name: port
params.description: The port number or service name. None means any.

params.name: family
params.description: Address family filter. 0 means any.

params.name: socktype
params.description: Socket type filter. 0 means any.

params.name: proto
params.description: Protocol filter. 0 means any.

params.name: flags
params.description: Flags to pass to the underlying getaddrinfo() C function.

returns.description: A list of 5-tuples (family, socktype, proto, canonname, sockaddr).

functions.description: Translate the host/port argument into a sequence of 5-tuples containing all the necessary arguments for creating a socket connected to that service.

functions.name: gethostbyname

params.name: hostname
params.description: The host name to resolve.

returns.description: The IPv4 address as a string in dotted notation (e.g. '93.184.216.34').

functions.description: Translate a host name to IPv4 address format. The IPv4 address is returned as a string.

functions.name: gethostbyaddr

params.name: ip_address
params.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 address string.

returns.description: A triple (hostname, aliaslist, ipaddrlist) where hostname is the primary host name, aliaslist is a list of alternative host names, and ipaddrlist is a list of IPv4/v6 addresses.

functions.description: Return a triple (hostname, aliaslist, ipaddrlist) for the given IP address. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6.

functions.name: gethostname

returns.description: The hostname of the machine where the Python interpreter is currently executing.

functions.description: Return a string containing the hostname of the machine where the Python interpreter is currently executing.

functions.name: getfqdn

params.name: name
params.description: The name to look up. If empty or omitted, the local host is used.

returns.description: The fully qualified domain name.

functions.description: Return a fully qualified domain name for name. If the name is empty or omitted, it is interpreted as the local host.

functions.name: getservbyname

params.name: servicename
params.description: The service name (e.g. 'http', 'ftp').

params.name: protocolname
params.description: The protocol name (e.g. 'tcp', 'udp'). If omitted, any protocol will match.

returns.description: The port number for the service.

functions.description: Translate an Internet service name and protocol name to a port number.

functions.name: getservbyport

params.name: port
params.description: The port number.

params.name: protocolname
params.description: The protocol name (e.g. 'tcp', 'udp'). If omitted, any protocol will match.

returns.description: The service name for the port.

functions.description: Translate an Internet port number and protocol name to a service name.

functions.name: getprotobyname

params.name: protocolname
params.description: The protocol name (e.g. 'tcp', 'udp', 'icmp').

returns.description: The protocol number.

functions.description: Translate an Internet protocol name to a constant suitable for passing as the third argument to socket().

functions.name: setdefaulttimeout

params.name: timeout
params.description: The default timeout in seconds. None means no timeout (blocking mode).

returns.description: No return value.

functions.description: Set the default timeout in seconds for new socket objects. A value of None indicates that new socket objects have no timeout.

functions.name: getdefaulttimeout

returns.description: The default socket timeout in seconds, or None.

functions.description: Return the default timeout in seconds for new socket objects. None means new socket objects have no timeout.

functions.name: inet_aton

params.name: ip_string
params.description: An IPv4 address in dotted-quad string format (e.g. '192.168.1.1').

returns.description: The IPv4 address as a 32-bit packed binary string (4 bytes).

functions.description: Convert an IPv4 address from dotted-quad string format to 32-bit packed binary format.

functions.name: inet_ntoa

params.name: packed_ip
params.description: A 32-bit packed binary IPv4 address (4 bytes).

returns.description: The IPv4 address in dotted-quad string format.

functions.description: Convert a 32-bit packed IPv4 address to dotted-quad string format.

functions.name: inet_pton

params.name: address_family
params.description: AF_INET or AF_INET6.

params.name: ip_string
params.description: The IP address string.

returns.description: The packed binary address.

functions.description: Convert an IP address from its family-specific string format to packed binary format. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6.

functions.name: inet_ntop

params.name: address_family
params.description: AF_INET or AF_INET6.

params.name: packed_ip
params.description: The packed binary address.

returns.description: The IP address string.

functions.description: Convert a packed binary IP address to its string form. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6.

functions.name: htons

params.name: x
params.description: A 16-bit positive integer in host byte order.

returns.description: The value in network byte order (big-endian).

functions.description: Convert a 16-bit positive integer from host to network byte order.

functions.name: htonl

params.name: x
params.description: A 32-bit positive integer in host byte order.

returns.description: The value in network byte order (big-endian).

functions.description: Convert a 32-bit positive integer from host to network byte order.

functions.name: ntohs

params.name: x
params.description: A 16-bit positive integer in network byte order.

returns.description: The value in host byte order.

functions.description: Convert a 16-bit positive integer from network to host byte order.

functions.name: ntohl

params.name: x
params.description: A 32-bit positive integer in network byte order.

returns.description: The value in host byte order.

functions.description: Convert a 32-bit positive integer from network to host byte order.

classes.name: socket
classes.description: A socket object representing a network endpoint for communication. Created via socket.socket() constructor.

params.name: family
params.description: Address family. Defaults to AF_INET.

params.name: type
params.description: Socket type. Defaults to SOCK_STREAM.

params.name: proto
params.description: Protocol number. Defaults to 0.

methods.name: accept

returns.description: A pair (conn, address) where conn is a new socket object usable to send and receive data on the connection, and address is the address bound to the socket on the other end.

methods.description: Accept a connection. The socket must be bound to an address and listening for connections. The return value is a pair (conn, address).

methods.name: bind

params.name: address
params.description: The address to bind to. For AF_INET, a (host, port) pair. For AF_INET6, a (host, port, flowinfo, scopeid) tuple.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Bind the socket to address. The socket must not already be bound.

methods.name: close

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Close the socket. All future operations on the socket object will fail. The remote end will receive no more data.

methods.name: connect

params.name: address
params.description: The remote address. For AF_INET, a (host, port) pair.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Connect to a remote socket at address. Raises socket.error if the connection fails.

methods.name: connect_ex

params.name: address
params.description: The remote address. For AF_INET, a (host, port) pair.

returns.description: 0 on success, or the value of errno on failure.

methods.description: Like connect(), but return an error indicator instead of raising an exception. Returns 0 if the operation succeeded.

methods.name: fileno

returns.description: The file descriptor of the socket.

methods.description: Return the socket's file descriptor (a small integer). Useful for select.select().

methods.name: getpeername

returns.description: The remote address to which the socket is connected. For AF_INET, a (host, port) pair.

methods.description: Return the remote address to which the socket is connected.

methods.name: getsockname

returns.description: The socket's own address. For AF_INET, a (host, port) pair.

methods.description: Return the socket's own address.

methods.name: getsockopt

params.name: level
params.description: The option level (e.g. SOL_SOCKET).

params.name: optname
params.description: The option name (e.g. SO_REUSEADDR).

params.name: buflen
params.description: If present, the maximum length of the buffer to receive the option value. If absent, an integer option is assumed.

returns.description: The socket option value. An integer if buflen is absent, a buffer string otherwise.

methods.description: Return the value of the given socket option.

methods.name: setsockopt

params.name: level
params.description: The option level (e.g. SOL_SOCKET).

params.name: optname
params.description: The option name (e.g. SO_REUSEADDR).

params.name: value
params.description: The option value. An integer or a buffer (packed struct).

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Set the value of the given socket option.

methods.name: listen

params.name: backlog
params.description: The maximum number of queued connections. Should be at least 0; most systems silently limit it to at least 1.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Enable a server to accept connections. The backlog argument specifies the maximum number of queued connections.

methods.name: makefile

params.name: mode
params.description: File mode ('r', 'w', 'rw', 'rb', 'wb', etc.). Defaults to 'r'.

params.name: bufsize
params.description: Buffer size. 0 means unbuffered, 1 means line-buffered, negative means system default.

returns.description: A file object associated with the socket.

methods.description: Return a file object associated with the socket. The file object references a dup()ped version of the socket file descriptor.

methods.name: recv

params.name: bufsize
params.description: The maximum amount of data to receive at once.

params.name: flags
params.description: Optional flags (e.g. MSG_PEEK, MSG_WAITALL). Defaults to 0.

returns.description: The received data as a string. Empty string indicates the connection has been closed.

methods.description: Receive data from the socket. The return value is a string representing the data received.

methods.name: recvfrom

params.name: bufsize
params.description: The maximum amount of data to receive at once.

params.name: flags
params.description: Optional flags. Defaults to 0.

returns.description: A pair (data, address) where data is a string and address is the sender's address.

methods.description: Receive data from the socket. The return value is a pair (data, address) where data is the received data and address is the address of the socket sending the data.

methods.name: send

params.name: data
params.description: The data to send.

params.name: flags
params.description: Optional flags. Defaults to 0.

returns.description: The number of bytes sent. May be less than len(data).

methods.description: Send data to the socket. The socket must be connected. Returns the number of bytes sent, which may be less than the length of the data.

methods.name: sendall

params.name: data
params.description: The data to send.

params.name: flags
params.description: Optional flags. Defaults to 0.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Send data to the socket. Unlike send(), this method continues to send data until either all data has been sent or an error occurs.

methods.name: sendto

params.name: data
params.description: The data to send.

params.name: flags
params.description: Optional flags.

params.name: address
params.description: The destination address.

returns.description: The number of bytes sent.

methods.description: Send data to the socket. The socket should not be connected to a remote socket, since the destination socket is specified by address.

methods.name: setblocking

params.name: flag
params.description: If False, the socket is set to non-blocking mode. If True, set to blocking mode.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Set blocking or non-blocking mode of the socket. Equivalent to settimeout(None) for blocking and settimeout(0.0) for non-blocking.

methods.name: settimeout

params.name: value
params.description: Timeout in seconds. None means blocking mode. 0.0 means non-blocking mode.

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Set a timeout on blocking socket operations. A value of None puts the socket in blocking mode, 0.0 puts it in non-blocking mode.

methods.name: gettimeout

returns.description: The timeout in seconds, or None if no timeout is set.

methods.description: Return the timeout in seconds associated with socket operations, or None if no timeout is set.

methods.name: shutdown

params.name: how
params.description: SHUT_RD (no more reads), SHUT_WR (no more writes), or SHUT_RDWR (no more reads or writes).

returns.description: No return value.

methods.description: Shut down one or both halves of the connection.

classes.name: error
classes.description: Exception raised for socket-related errors. Subclass of IOError.

classes.name: timeout
classes.description: Exception raised when a socket operation times out. Subclass of socket.error.

classes.name: herror
classes.description: Exception raised for address-related errors (host not found, etc.). Subclass of socket.error.

classes.name: gaierror
classes.description: Exception raised for getaddrinfo() and getnameinfo() related errors. Subclass of socket.error.

constants.name: AF_INET
constants.description: Address family for IPv4 addresses. The most common address family.

constants.name: AF_UNIX
constants.description: Address family for Unix domain sockets. Used for local inter-process communication.

constants.name: SOCK_STREAM
constants.description: Socket type for TCP (reliable, connection-oriented byte streams).

constants.name: SOCK_DGRAM
constants.description: Socket type for UDP (unreliable, connectionless datagrams).

constants.name: SOCK_RAW
constants.description: Socket type for raw sockets. Allows access to the underlying transport protocol.

constants.name: SOL_SOCKET
constants.description: Socket option level for socket-level options.

constants.name: SO_REUSEADDR
constants.description: Socket option to allow reuse of local addresses.

constants.name: SO_KEEPALIVE
constants.description: Socket option to enable sending of keep-alive messages on connection-oriented sockets.

constants.name: SHUT_RD
constants.description: Constant for shutdown(): further receives are disallowed.

constants.name: SHUT_WR
constants.description: Constant for shutdown(): further sends are disallowed.

constants.name: SHUT_RDWR
constants.description: Constant for shutdown(): further sends and receives are disallowed.

constants.name: has_ipv6
constants.description: True if the platform supports IPv6.
